g.text: attention lets points "vote" on the shared circle.
• term 1: squared error of the predicted centre vector (regression)
Implemented as: mean over points of (vector MSE) + (BCE), then summed.
d_model must be divisible by nhead (multi-head
attention splits d_model into nhead slices) and
1-6 & 8, but NOT 7 -> sweep bumps heads=7 to 126.
centre constrained so the circle is fully inside
• feature c = grayscale (circle vs background intensity)
• optional Gaussian noise on c and random "clutter" points
• ground truth per point: vector to centre + inside/outside label
A FRESH random scene is drawn every batch (infinite stream,
for each epoch: draw fresh batch -> forward -> loss
-> backward -> Adam.step(). Keeps a deque of the last
100 epoch losses; every 100 epochs records both
loss_avg and loss_min for that window.
Cartesian product of layers x heads. One `python train.py`
subprocess per cell. resolve_d_model() picks the nearest
--skip-existing skips cells that already have metrics.json
(safe to re-run / resume); a failed cell is logged and the
Loads a trained run, computes test loss on a fixed-seed set,
and (--visualize) saves N annotated plots with per-example
metrics: centre-localisation error, vector MSE, classification
accuracy. Estimated centre = mean(point + predicted vector).
Task: given a set of 2D points (x, y, grayscale c), the model predicts —
for every point independently — a vector to the circle's centre and the
probability that the point lies inside the circle.
